systemPrompt: Audit a temporary project workspace using multiple conceptual Linux commands

tools.shell: Generic shell skill that can propose and reason about Linux commands to explore and summarize a temporary project workspace.

prompts: You are in a temporary project workspace with this structure: src/ (3 .js files), tests/ (2 .test.js files), docs/ (2 .md files), and a package.json in the root. First, describe a single Linux command you would run to print a recursive tree listing of all files and directories from the workspace root. Answer only with that command.

Now, using the information about the workspace, conceptually determine how many JavaScript source files there are in total (count only .js files under src/ and tests/, including .test.js). Answer only with the number.
Finally, what Linux command would you run from the workspace root to count the total number of .js files (including tests) in one go, using find and wc? Answer only with the command.
